title: Elijah and Elisha
description: A modest prophetic route linking key settings in the ministries of Elijah and Elisha.
caution: This route is a narrative outline. Several prophetic locations are symbolic or approximate rather than exact archaeological sites.

stops.name: Samaria
stops.description: The royal center in the northern kingdom.
stops.notes: A major prophetic stage in the narrative.

stops.name: Mount Carmel
stops.description: The contest site between Elijah and the prophets of Baal.
stops.notes: One of the clearer geographic references in the story.

stops.name: Horeb
stops.description: The mountain of the still small voice.
stops.notes: Often treated as the same mountain tradition as Sinai.

stops.name: Jericho
stops.description: The city where Elijah and Elisha depart together.
stops.notes: A narrative junction before Elijah's departure.

stops.name: Gilgal
stops.description: A recurring prophetic stop before the whirlwind departure.
stops.notes: The name may refer to more than one locality.

segments.label: Prophetic confrontation
segments.description: Elijah moves toward the royal court.

segments.label: To Carmel
segments.description: The dramatic contest at Mount Carmel.
segments.caution: A direct line summarizes the narrative movement.

segments.label: Into the wilderness
segments.caution: The exact path is not specified.

segments.label: Return north
segments.description: The prophetic succession narrative turns back toward the Jordan valley.
